rulings.Case Title: JOANNA L. PFEISTER VS. GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Supplemental Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for October 9, 2025, line 1. Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to File a Supplemental Complaint is GRANTED. Supplemental Complaint shall be filed and served forthwith. The Court makes no determination as to the viability of the supplemental complaint at this stage. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOHANNA METZGAR VS. BRIAN KENDALL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement (Ccp 664.6)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for October 9, 2025, line 2. Plaintiff's Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement (CCP 664.6) is GRANTED. Defendant must set up access accounts for Plaintiff to confirm that required life insurance policy remains in place. Access shall be available no later than October 15, 2025. Fees are granted in the amount of $24,247.50. Late-filed supplemental briefing and declarations by the defendant are stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LEE GREENBERG VS. JERRY BENJAMIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for October 9, 2025, line 4. Defendant Karen Benjamin's Motion to Set Aside Default/Default Judgment/Leave to Defend is DENIED. Moving party fails to meet the burden for discretionary relief under CCP 473(b). Shapell Socal Rental Properties, LLC v. Chico's FAS, Inc. (2022) 85 Cal.App.5th 198 is inapposite. See Kelly Declaration and e-mail communications attached thereto. Request for CCP 128.7 sanctions is DENIED. No showing of compliance with CCP 128.7(c)(1). The Court notes that 5 court days before the hearing defendant filed a document titled "Motion for Relief from Default..." Notwithstanding its title the document appears to be a reply (not a separate motion) and is considered as such. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
